story_summary: This comprehensive test suite validates the password reset functionality for registered users, ensuring compliance with security policies, privacy requirements, user experience standards, and operational reliability. It covers the happy path scenarios for successful reset requests and password changes, boundary and edge cases for input validation, token expiry, concurrency, and rate limiting, as well as security validations guarding against injection attacks, cross-site scripting, CSRF, phishing, and ensuring secure protocols and audit logging are enforced. Each test case preserves its original detailed descriptions, preconditions, steps, expected outcomes, priority, and tagging to guarantee a thorough and robust validation of password reset features across various conditions and security angles.

test_cases.title: Successful Password Reset Request and Email Delivery
test_cases.description: Verify that a registered user can successfully initiate a password reset request and receive a password reset email containing a unique, secure link with clear instructions.
preconditions: User is registered with the system and has an active account.
User has access to the registered email address.

steps.action: Navigate to the login page and select 'Forgot Password' option.
steps.expected_result: The password reset request form is displayed prompting for email input.

steps.action: Enter the registered email address into the password reset form and submit the request.
steps.expected_result: System accepts the request and displays a generic confirmation message without revealing user registration status.

steps.action: Check the registered email inbox for a new message from the system.
steps.expected_result: A password reset email is received promptly containing a secure HTTPS reset link, clear instructions for resetting the password, and security advice.

steps.action: Verify the reset link in the email is a unique URL with a token and uses HTTPS.
steps.expected_result: Reset link is unique, unguessable, and uses HTTPS to ensure secure transmission.

test_cases.expected_outcome: User successfully initiates a password reset request and receives a timely email with a valid, secure password reset link containing clear instructions.

test_cases.title: Successful Password Reset Using Valid Link
test_cases.description: Verify that a registered user can reset their password by clicking a valid password reset link and entering a new password that complies with security policies.
preconditions: User has received a password reset email with a valid, unexpired, unused reset link.
User has access to the device to open the reset link.

steps.action: Click the password reset link from the received email within the validity period.
steps.expected_result: The system validates the authenticity and expiry status of the link and directs the user to the password reset page.

steps.action: Enter a new password into the password field that complies with all password policies (e.g., minimum length, complexity).
steps.expected_result: Password input field shows masked characters, confirms compliance with security policies.

steps.action: Confirm the new password by entering the same password in the confirmation field and submit the form.
steps.expected_result: System validates that both password fields match and comply with policies.

steps.action: Submit the new password for processing.
steps.expected_result: System updates the user account with the new password, invalidates the used reset link, and logs the change.

steps.action: Observe the notification displayed by the system after the password update.
steps.expected_result: User receives a confirmation message indicating the password was successfully reset and can now log in using the new password.

test_cases.expected_outcome: User successfully resets their password using the valid reset link and is informed of completion, enabling easy login with the new password.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Request with Multiple Valid Reset Links Active
test_cases.description: Verify that when a registered user requests multiple password resets in a short period, multiple valid reset links remain active unless expired or used, adhering to rate limiting and security policies.
preconditions: User is registered and logged out.
User has access to registered email address.

steps.action: Submit a password reset request using the registered email.
steps.expected_result: System accepts the request and sends a password reset email with a unique link.

steps.action: Within a short time (less than expiry period), submit a second password reset request using the same email.
steps.expected_result: System accepts the second request and sends another password reset email with a different unique link.

steps.action: Check both password reset emails have been received.
steps.expected_result: Both emails are delivered with distinct, valid HTTPS reset links and clear instructions.

steps.action: Use the first reset link to successfully reset the password following the standard flow.
steps.expected_result: The first link is accepted, password is reset, and the link becomes invalid after use.

steps.action: Attempt to use the second reset link after the first reset.
steps.expected_result: The second link remains valid if within expiry and unused, allowing for password reset again if needed.

test_cases.expected_outcome: User can request multiple password reset links which remain valid independently, system enforces rate limiting and security without invalidating existing valid links.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Request with Non-Registered Email Provides Generic Feedback
test_cases.description: Verify that when a password reset is requested using an email not registered with the system, the system responds with a generic message that does not reveal the non-existence of the account, protecting user privacy.
preconditions: Email address used is not registered in the system.

steps.action: Navigate to the password reset request form.
Enter an unregistered email address and submit the password reset request.
steps.expected_result: System accepts the request and displays a generic confirmation message indicating instructions were sent if the email is registered.

steps.action: Check the inbox and spam/junk folders of the unregistered email address.
steps.expected_result: No password reset email is received.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System protects privacy by providing the same feedback for registered and unregistered emails during reset requests, preventing disclosure of account existence.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Email Contains Clear Instructions and Security Advice
test_cases.description: Verify that the password reset email sent to the user includes clear instructions on how to reset the password, the secure reset link, and security advice to protect the account.
preconditions: User requests a password reset using a registered email.

steps.action: Submit a valid password reset request with a registered email address.
steps.expected_result: System sends a password reset email.

steps.action: Open the received password reset email and review its content.
steps.expected_result: Email contains a secure HTTPS reset link, step-by-step instructions for resetting the password, advice about the link expiry time, warnings not to share the link, and contact information for support.

steps.action: Check that the email template complies with branding and accessibility standards (e.g., proper logos, readable fonts, alt text for images).
steps.expected_result: Email complies with branding and accessibility guidelines.

test_cases.expected_outcome: User receives a well-structured, clear, and accessible password reset email with all necessary information to securely proceed with resetting their password.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Request with Unregistered Email Address
test_cases.description: Verify system behavior when a password reset request is made using an email address not registered in the system.
preconditions: No account exists with the test email address.

steps.action: Navigate to the password reset page.
Enter an unregistered email address (e.g., unregistered@example.com).
steps.expected_result: System does not indicate whether the email is registered or not.

steps.action: Submit the password reset request.
steps.expected_result: A generic message like "If this email is registered, a reset link will be sent" is displayed.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System protects user privacy by not disclosing existence of account information.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Link Expiry Boundary Test
test_cases.description: Confirm the system invalidates the password reset link exactly after the expiration period (e.g., 60 minutes).
preconditions: Registered user exists with an active password reset link generated exactly 60 minutes ago.

steps.action: Wait exactly 60 minutes (or manipulate system time for testing).
steps.expected_result: The system treats the link as expired and invalid.

steps.action: Attempt to use the password reset link.
steps.expected_result:
An error message is shown prompting the user to request a new reset.
The user cannot proceed to enter a new password.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Reset links become unusable immediately after expiration time.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Link Reuse Attempt
test_cases.description: Verify that once a password reset link has been used, it cannot be reused.
preconditions: Registered user has a valid password reset link and has successfully reset their password.

steps.action: Use the password reset link to reset the password.
steps.expected_result: The system accepts the link and resets the password.

steps.action: Log out if applicable.
Attempt to use the same password reset link again.
steps.expected_result:
The system rejects the link as invalid or already used.
An error message appears instructing to initiate a new password reset request.
No password reset form is presented.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Used reset links are invalidated and cannot be reused.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Request Rate Limiting Boundary Test
test_cases.description: Test the system's rate limiting on password reset requests to prevent abuse.
preconditions: Registered user exists.
Password reset rate limit policy defined (e.g., max 5 requests per hour).

steps.action: Submit the password reset request with the registered email 5 times consecutively within one hour.
steps.expected_result: The first 5 requests receive normal processing and email dispatch.

steps.action: Submit a 6th request within the same hour.
steps.expected_result:
The 6th request is rejected or delayed with a message such as "Too many requests, please try again later." 
No new email is sent after the limit is exceeded.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Rate limiting effectively prevents excessive password reset requests.

test_cases.title: Password Reset with Minimum and Maximum Length Passwords
test_cases.description: Validate that new passwords exactly at the minimum and maximum allowed lengths are accepted or rejected accordingly.
preconditions: Registered user has a valid, non-expired password reset link ready for use.
System enforces minimum and maximum password lengths (e.g., min 8, max 64 characters).

steps.action: Use the reset link to access the new password form.
Enter and confirm a password exactly 8 characters long that meets complexity requirements.
Repeat steps 1-2 with a password exactly 64 characters long that meets complexity requirements.
Repeat with passwords shorter than 8 and longer than 64 characters.
steps.expected_result: Passwords rejected with clear error messages.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password length boundaries are respected and validated correctly.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Link URL Length and Encoding Boundary Test
test_cases.description: Assess system handling of unusually long or altered password reset URLs and related encoding issues.
preconditions: Registered user has a valid password reset link.

steps.action: Copy the password reset URL.
Append additional URL parameters or extend the query string with valid and invalid characters (e.g., very long strings, Unicode, special symbols).
Attempt to use the modified URL.
steps.expected_result:
System validates the core reset token correctly regardless of additional parameters or rejects if token corrupted.
No system errors or crashes occur due to URL length or encoding issues.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Robust handling of password reset URL variations without security or functional compromise.

test_cases.title: Concurrent Multiple Password Reset Link Usage
test_cases.description: Test concurrent access and usage of multiple valid password reset links issued to the same user.
preconditions: Registered user requests multiple password resets, resulting in multiple valid reset tokens with overlapping validity.

steps.action: Request password reset twice in succession. Both emails contain valid reset links.
steps.expected_result: Both reset emails received with valid links.

steps.action: Open both reset links in separate browser sessions simultaneously.
Use the first link to reset the password successfully.
steps.expected_result: Password reset successful with first link.

steps.action: Attempt to use the second link afterward.
steps.expected_result:
Second link becomes invalid and is rejected.
Appropriate messages shown.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Earlier used reset links invalidate subsequent tokens if policy requires or system handles concurrent resets safely.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Email Non-Delivery or Delay Handling
test_cases.description: Test system behavior and user feedback when the password reset email is not delivered promptly or fails due to email service issues.
preconditions: Email service is down or email to user is blocked (simulate via a mock or email fail condition).

steps.action: Submit a password reset request with a registered email.
Simulate delay or failure in email sending.
Observe system response and user notifications.
steps.expected_result:
System provides generic message avoiding information leakage.
Logs failure and retries as per policy.
UI remains responsive.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Robust error handling and clear user communication during email delivery issues.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Submission with Empty or Blank Password Fields
test_cases.description: Verify system validation when user submits an empty or whitespace-only new password and confirmation.
preconditions: Registered user has a valid password reset link.

steps.action: Access the password reset form via a valid link.
Leave the new password and confirm password fields empty or filled with spaces.
Submit the form.
steps.expected_result:
System rejects submission with clear validation errors.
Password not updated.
Form remains available for correction.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Input validation prevents empty or invalid password submission.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Link Access over Insecure HTTP Protocol
test_cases.description: Verify system behavior when a user attempts to access the password reset link via HTTP instead of HTTPS.
preconditions: Registered user has a valid reset link using HTTPS.

steps.action: Modify the reset link URL to use HTTP protocol.
Attempt to access the link via HTTP connection.
steps.expected_result:
System either redirects to HTTPS or rejects with error emphasizing secure connection.
Reset link functionality disabled or blocked over HTTP.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset functionality is only accessible via secure HTTPS, protecting sensitive token transmission.

test_cases.title: Password Reset with Passwords Failing Complexity Rules
test_cases.description: Verify system rejection of new passwords that do not comply with complexity requirements (e.g., missing uppercase, digits, special chars).
preconditions: Valid reset link available.
Password policy enforces complexity rules.

steps.action: Access password reset form with valid link.
Enter passwords that lack required complexity (e.g., all lowercase, no digits, no special characters).
Submit the form.
steps.expected_result:
System rejects such passwords with clear feedback.
Password not updated.

test_cases.expected_outcome: System enforces password policy strictly during reset.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Link Expiry During Reset Process
test_cases.description: Test behavior if the password reset link expires while the user is entering a new password.
preconditions: User initiates reset and opens the reset form just before expiry (e.g., at 59 minutes of a 60-minute limit).

steps.action: Navigate to password reset form using the valid link near expiration time.
Delay input for over the expiration period (e.g., wait past 1 minute).
Submit the new password form.
steps.expected_result: System detects link expiry, prevents update, shows expired link message, prompts for new request.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Expired links lead to reset failure even if form was initially accessed.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Log Audit Validation
test_cases.description: Verify that password reset requests and reset actions are logged with correct details for auditing.
preconditions: System logging and audit feature enabled.

steps.action: Request password reset for a registered user.
Complete password reset process successfully.
Access logs or audit records.
steps.expected_result: Audit logs accessible and show correct entries without sensitive data.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Adequate logging exists for security and compliance purposes.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Request with Excessively Long Email Address
test_cases.description: Verify system handling of extremely long email addresses during password reset request.
preconditions: Email field in password reset form allows input; test email exceeds typical email length limits (e.g., 254 chars).

steps.action: Enter an excessively long but syntactically valid email address in the reset form.
Submit the request.
steps.expected_result:
System validates length and format, rejects if length exceeded with meaningful error.
No account existence revealed.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Input validation handles outlier email lengths gracefully.

test_cases.title: Password Reset Email Content and Accessibility Compliance Check
test_cases.description: Verify that the reset email contains all required elements and meets accessibility standards.
preconditions: Registered user requests a password reset.

steps.action: Capture the sent reset email.
Inspect email content for presence of: reset link (HTTPS), instructions, security advice, branding elements.
Use accessibility tools to verify compliance (e.g., alt text for images, readable font size, color contrast).

test_cases.expected_outcome: Clear, secure, and accessible password reset emails are sent to users.

test_cases.title: Prevent SQL Injection in Password Reset Request
test_cases.description: Validate that the password reset request form input fields properly sanitize and parameterize inputs to prevent SQL Injection attacks.
preconditions: User is on the password reset request page.

steps.action: Enter malicious SQL injection payload (e.g., ' OR '1'='1') into the email address input field.
Submit the password reset request.
Observe the system's response.
steps.expected_result:
System treats input as a plain string and does not execute any injected SQL commands.
No sensitive data leakage or error messages revealing database structure.
System shows a generic response that does not reveal whether the email is registered.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset request is safely processed without SQL injection vulnerability exposure.

test_cases.title: Prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Password Reset Email Input
test_cases.description: Ensure inputs accepted during password reset request are properly sanitized and encoded to prevent stored or reflected XSS attacks.
preconditions: User is on the password reset request page.

steps.action: Input a script tag or XSS payload (e.g., <script>alert('XSS')</script>) into the email field.
Submit the password reset form.
Verify the system response and any output displayed back to the user.
steps.expected_result:
System encodes or strips HTML tags and does not execute injected scripts.
No alert pop-ups or script execution occurs in the browser.
System response remains generic without disclosing account existence.

test_cases.expected_outcome: The input does not result in cross-site scripting vulnerabilities on the reset page or confirmation pages.

test_cases.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) Protection on Password Reset Request
test_cases.description: Verify that submitting a password reset request requires a valid CSRF token to prevent CSRF attacks.
preconditions: User is on the password reset request page with a valid session.

steps.action: Capture the password reset request form submission.
Attempt to submit the form without the CSRF token or with an invalid token (e.g., using a custom tool or script).
steps.expected_result: Submission attempted without token or with invalid token.

steps.action: Observe the server’s response.
steps.expected_result:
Server rejects requests missing a valid CSRF token with an error or forbidden status.
Password reset request is not processed without valid CSRF validation.

test_cases.expected_outcome: CSRF protection blocks unauthorized form submissions for password reset requests.

test_cases.title: Rate Limiting on Password Reset Requests to Prevent Abuse
test_cases.description: Ensure the system enforces rate limiting on password reset requests to prevent brute force or denial of service attacks.
preconditions: Ability to repeatedly send password reset requests to the same or different email addresses.

steps.action: Send multiple password reset requests (e.g., more than 5) for the same email address within one minute.
Send multiple requests for multiple different email addresses rapidly.
Observe system response and any throttling or blocking behavior.
steps.expected_result:
System limits the number of password reset requests per IP address and/or per target email within a defined time window.
Beyond threshold, system responds with a generic error or throttling message without disclosing account validity.
No password reset emails are sent after limit is exceeded.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Rate limiting is enforced to mitigate abuse or brute force attack risks via password reset functionality.

test_cases.title: Reject Oversized Payloads on Password Reset Request Form
test_cases.description: Validate that password reset request inputs reject oversized payloads to prevent denial of service or buffer overflow attacks.
preconditions: User is on the password reset request page.

steps.action: Input an excessively long string (e.g., 10,000+ characters) into the email address field.
Submit the password reset request.
Observe system behavior and response.
steps.expected_result:
System rejects the request with input validation error stating input size exceeded.
The system does not crash, slow down significantly, or expose sensitive information.
No emails are sent for invalid inputs.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset system properly validates input length and safely handles oversized inputs.

test_cases.title: Ensure Password Reset Link Uses Secure HTTPS Protocol
test_cases.description: Verify that password reset links generated and sent via email use HTTPS scheme only to protect confidentiality and integrity during transmission.
preconditions: Trigger a password reset email for a registered user.

steps.action: Request password reset for a registered email.
Retrieve the password reset email content.
Inspect the reset link URL in the email.

test_cases.expected_outcome:
The reset link uses https:// scheme exclusively.
No plain http:// links are present.
The link contains a strong, unguessable token parameter.
Password reset links ensure secure communication channel enforcement with HTTPS.

test_cases.title: Validate Password Reset Link Token Has Expiry and Is Single-Use
test_cases.description: Confirm the password reset token in the link expires as per policy and becomes invalid after one use to avoid replay attacks.
preconditions: A valid password reset link has been sent.

steps.action: Use the reset link to successfully reset the password within the allowed timeframe.
Attempt to reuse the same reset link again after successful reset.
steps.expected_result: Link rejected as invalid or used.

steps.action: Attempt to use an expired token by waiting beyond expiry time and then accessing the link.
steps.expected_result: Expired token results in error and prompts new reset request.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Reset tokens are securely managed with proper expiry and single-use enforcement.

test_cases.title: Input Validation and Security on New Password Entry
test_cases.description: Verify that the new password and confirmation fields accept only inputs that comply with password policy and sanitize inputs to prevent injection or XSS.
preconditions: User has accessed the password reset page via a valid link.

steps.action: Enter a password that violates the password policy (e.g., too short, missing complexity).
Enter a password containing script tags or malicious payload.
Attempt to submit mismatched password and confirmation.
Submit a valid password complying with policy.

test_cases.expected_outcome:
System rejects passwords not meeting policy with clear errors.
Malicious input sanitized; no script execution.
Mismatch rejected with error.
Valid password accepted and reset proceeds.

test_cases.title: No Account Enumeration Via Password Reset Request Responses
test_cases.description: Ensure the password reset request response is identical regardless of whether the email is registered, preventing attackers from enumerating valid accounts.
preconditions: User is on the password reset request page.

steps.action: Submit a reset request with an email known to be registered.
Submit a reset request with an email known to be unregistered.
Compare system responses and timings of both requests.
steps.expected_result: System responses identical and timing indistinguishable.

test_cases.expected_outcome:
Both responses show generic messages without account info.
No timing or detail leaks.
User privacy protected against enumeration.

test_cases.title: Email Content Security and Phishing Protection Validation
test_cases.description: Verify the password reset email content does not contain malicious links, clearly states instructions, and includes security advice to help users avoid phishing attacks.
preconditions: A password reset email is sent successfully.

steps.action: Retrieve the content of the reset email.
Check that the link URL matches the official domain and uses HTTPS.
Verify the email contains clear instructions on resetting password.
Confirm presence of security advice (e.g., 'Do not share this link', 'If you did not request this, ignore this email').
Ensure branding and accessibility compliance.

test_cases.expected_outcome:
Reset link points to legitimate HTTPS site.
Instructions clear and user safe.
Email template conforms to branding and accessibility.
No suspicious links present.

test_cases.title: Logging and Audit Trail Verification for Password Reset Actions
test_cases.description: Verify that all password reset requests, email sends, link accesses, and password changes are logged for audit and forensic purposes without sensitive data exposure.
preconditions: Ability to trigger password reset process and observe logs.

steps.action: Initiate a password reset request for a registered user.
Access the reset link.
Complete the password reset with a compliant password.
Review audit logs for entries corresponding to each action.
steps.expected_result:
Logs show timestamp, user ID/email hash, IP, action type.
No plaintext passwords or tokens logged.
Logs protected against unauthorized access.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password reset operations fully logged securely for auditing.

test_cases.title: Masking Password Fields and Preventing Password Caching
test_cases.description: Ensure password input fields during reset mask user input and prevent browser autofill or caching to protect password confidentiality.
preconditions: User is on the password reset form page.

steps.action: Inspect password and confirm password input fields for type attribute and autocomplete settings.
steps.expected_result: Fields use type='password' and autocomplete disabled or 'new-password'.

steps.action: Type new password characters and verify they are masked.
Attempt to use browser autofill for these input fields.
Submit new password successfully.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Password inputs maintain privacy and security during entry.

test_cases.title: Error Handling and User Feedback Security During Email Sending Failures
test_cases.description: Validate that system returns secure, user-friendly error messages during failures such as inability to send password reset email, without revealing internal system information.
preconditions: Simulate failure in email service (e.g., SMTP down).

steps.action: Initiate password reset request with a valid registered email.
Observe the response when the email sending process fails.
Check for any error messages displayed to user.
steps.expected_result:
Generic, polite notification about inability to send email.
No internal error information or stack traces shown.

test_cases.expected_outcome: Email sending failures handled gracefully and securely from user perspective.
